name: dev-server
context: Start and configure Vite's dev server — host, port, proxy, HMR, HTTPS, CORS, warmup, middleware mode, and file system restrictions
info: Vite's dev server is the core development experience, powered by native ES modules and instant Hot Module Replacement. Configuration lives under the `server` key in vite.config.ts. `server.host` defaults to localhost; set to `0.0.0.0` or `true` to expose on all network interfaces (required for Docker/WSL2). `server.port` defaults to 5173; if taken Vite auto-increments unless `server.strictPort` is true. `server.https` enables TLS and HTTP/2 — pass key/cert paths or use `@vitejs/plugin-basic-ssl` for self-signed certs. `server.open` auto-opens the browser on startup (accepts a path string). `server.proxy` defines custom proxy rules for API backends, supporting path rewriting, regex matching, WebSocket proxying, and per-route `configure` hooks — powered by http-proxy under the hood. `server.cors` controls CORS headers (defaults to allowing all origins). `server.headers` sets custom response headers on all served files. `server.hmr` configures the HMR WebSocket connection: protocol (ws/wss), host, port, clientPort (for reverse proxies), overlay (error overlay toggle), and timeout. `server.warmup` accepts `clientFiles` and `ssrFiles` glob arrays to pre-transform frequently used modules at startup for faster initial loads. `server.watch` passes options to chokidar; by default `.git/` and `node_modules/` are excluded. `server.middlewareMode` disables the built-in HTTP server so Vite can be mounted as middleware in Express, Connect, Koa, or Fastify apps — essential for custom SSR setups. `server.fs.strict` (default true in Vite 6+) restricts file serving to the project root and workspace; `server.fs.allow` adds extra allowed directories; `server.fs.deny` blocks specific patterns (defaults to dotfiles). `server.origin` sets the origin of served asset URLs during dev. `server.allowedHosts` restricts which hostnames the server responds to, preventing DNS rebinding attacks. `server.forwardConsole` (experimental) forwards server-side console logs to the browser.
triggers: run vite on a different port

sections.title: Starting the dev server
sections.body: Run `vite`, `vite dev`, or `vite serve` to start the development server. Default port is 5173; it auto-increments if taken unless `server.strictPort` is set. Common CLI flags: `--host` (network exposure), `--port` (custom port), `--open` (auto-open browser), `--force` (re-bundle deps), `--config <file>` (custom config). The server uses native ES modules and only transforms files on demand, giving sub-second cold starts even on large projects.

sections.title: Proxy configuration for API backends
sections.body: Define proxy rules under `server.proxy` in [[vite/config]]. Supports simple string targets (`'/api': 'http://localhost:4000'`), path rewriting with `rewrite`, WebSocket proxying with `ws: true`, regex path matching, custom headers via `configure` hook, and `secure: false` for self-signed backend certs. Powered by http-proxy. Only applies during development — configure your reverse proxy (nginx, Caddy) for production.

sections.title: Hot Module Replacement (HMR)
sections.body: HMR updates changed modules in the browser without a full reload. Works out of the box for CSS, Vue SFCs, React (via [[vite/plugins]]), and Svelte. Configure the HMR WebSocket connection via `server.hmr`: set `protocol` (ws/wss), `host`, `port`, `clientPort` (for reverse proxies), `overlay` (error overlay), and `timeout`. Disable entirely with `server.hmr: false`. Common issues: firewall blocking WebSocket, reverse proxy not forwarding upgrade requests, missing framework plugin creating no HMR boundary.

sections.title: HTTPS and HTTP/2
sections.body: Enable HTTPS by passing `key` and `cert` to `server.https`. Generate trusted local certs with mkcert (`mkcert -install && mkcert localhost`). For quick self-signed certs, use the `@vitejs/plugin-basic-ssl` plugin. When HTTPS is enabled, Vite automatically upgrades to HTTP/2. HTTPS is required for testing Service Workers, Secure Cookies, and APIs that enforce secure contexts.

sections.title: Middleware mode for Express/Connect
sections.body: Set `server.middlewareMode: true` and `appType: 'custom'` to use Vite as middleware in an existing Node.js server. Access the connect-compatible middleware stack via `vite.middlewares`. Use `vite.transformIndexHtml()` and `vite.ssrLoadModule()` for custom SSR pipelines. Vite does not start its own HTTP server in this mode. Call `vite.close()` on shutdown. Works with Express, Connect, Koa (via adapter), and Fastify.

sections.title: File system serving restrictions
sections.body: Vite restricts file serving to prevent exposing sensitive files. `server.fs.strict` (default true) limits serving to the project root and workspace root. Add extra directories with `server.fs.allow`. Block specific patterns with `server.fs.deny` (dotfiles blocked by default). The deny list is checked before allow. In monorepos, workspace root is auto-detected and allowed. Symlinked dependencies are also allowed by default.

sections.title: Warmup for faster initial loads
sections.body: Pre-transform frequently used modules at server start with `server.warmup.clientFiles` and `server.warmup.ssrFiles`. Accepts file paths or glob patterns. Reduces the transformation waterfall on first page load by processing modules before the browser requests them. Only warm up files that are imported early in the module graph — warming too many files wastes startup time.

scripts.name: Start Dev Server
scripts.description: Start the Vite dev server with configurable host, port, and browser-open behavior

PROJECT_DIR.description: Path to the Vite project root

host.description: Server host — use 0.0.0.0 for network exposure

proxy_target.description: Backend URL to proxy API requests to (e.g. http://localhost:4000)

https.description: Enable HTTPS with auto-generated self-signed cert

url.description: Full URL the dev server is listening on (e.g. http://localhost:5173)

port.description: Actual port the server bound to (may differ if auto-incremented)

depends.reason: Server options are set in vite.config.ts under the server key

author: dojo-community
